title: Glandularia lambertii
intro: Glandularia lambertii, also known as Rose Verbena or Purple Sage, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Verbenaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and research has confirmed its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Glandularia lambertii include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Glandularia lambertii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support respiratory health and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Glandularia lambertii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. It belongs to the genus Glandularia, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas. The genus Glandularia is further divided into several species, with Glandularia lambertii being one of the species within this group. Glandularia lambertii is often referred to as the 'Rosita' or 'Purple Sage', and it is native to South America. Its classification as a flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae reflects its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
